title: stichorkis calcicola
intro: Stichorkis calcicola is a species of orchid native to Papua New Guinea, characterized by its small, white flowers and slender stem. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Stichorkis calcicola include its potential to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable asset in traditional herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Stichorkis calcicola often involve boiling the roots or leaves in water to create a tea or infusion for medicinal use.
